free_text: Reaction and proton-removal cross sections of 6Li,
An 80 MeV/nucleon 20Ne beam was fragmented on a
300 mg/cm2 9Be target which produced secondary beams.
Si detector telescope. The first two
with thickness 0.2 mm, lateral dimensions 2.5 cm x
2.5 cm, and 60 cm separation. The next five detectors,
were about 0.5 mm thick and had active areas from 300

Silicon as detector and target (see above)
The main experimental uncertainties are statistical in
origin: the counts in the reaction region, subtraction
of contaminant events, and extrapolation to the
nonreacting-projectile peak center. Events above the
peak are believed to be mainly pileup. If treated
similarly to those below the peak, they would add
about 1.5%, or 0.02 b, to the cross section. Given the
uncertain origin of these events, we make no
correction to cross section but add 2% uncertainty
in quadrature to the experimental uncertainties.
